website.summary: Todd Brown is a highly regarded copywriter and marketing expert, often referred to as a leading authority in engineering profitable customer acquisition campaigns. He is known for his expertise in direct response marketing and is sought after by other marketing professionals.

books.title: E5 Method Book
books.summary: What you're about to discover is a new and very different marketing method that generates steady and predictable sales for you from new customers and clients… Regardless of your product… Regardless of your price… And regardless of your marketplace. A Bold statement, I know. This “missing piece” of your marketing puzzle, turns your prospects desire for a result… into an intense buying demand for your specific product or service… without any selling. In fact, as you'll see, it works without you ever even talking about your product or service. What I'm about to share with you is NOT theory. It's the same marketing method taught to thousands of entrepreneurs that's generating daily sales for hundreds of different types of products and services for marketers today in 45 different countries around the globe… …who are launching and growing exciting businesses in over 65 different markets and niches online.

issues.content: The article discusses an incident where an umbrella flew away during an interview at the West Palm Beach Hilton, and uses this anecdote to illustrate the importance of perseverance despite obstacles. It also touches on choosing action verbs to convey ease of product use and unique ways of executing effective marketing strategies, such as presenting social proof beyond just testimonials.

This issue announces the opening of registration for Todd Brown's two-day Copywriting Workshop on September 20th. He explains his decision to share his copywriting techniques after two decades, citing two main reasons: to benefit his highest-level clients and to provide tactical nuances of copy that produce consistent sales at scale, which he feels is missing from most conceptual copywriting training. The 'My Favs This Week' section includes articles on subscription growth models, the 'Two-Hour Rule' for creative projects, and incorporating direct mail into marketing funnels. The 'Swipe of the Week' features a multi-page ad from John Carlton, highlighting his use of curiosity, new mechanisms, story, and proof. 'What I'm Tellin' My Team' discusses elements of good copy, using Gary Halbert's Personals Ad as an example, focusing on conversational tone, unique voice, simple language, details, character, and damaging admissions. The 'Rockstar Resource' recommends 'Scientific Advertising' by Claude Hopkins, and the 'Pic of the Week' shows a comic book called 'Foreskin Man.'

This memo opens with a quote from Teddy Roosevelt's 'The Man In The Arena' speech, which Todd Brown keeps in his office. 'My Favs This Week' includes articles on rules for productive creatives by Ryan Holiday, a step-by-step guide for YouTube Ads in 2021, and a Twitter thread on growing on Twitter by investing an hour a day. The issue also reminds readers about the upcoming Copy Workshop with limited seats. The 'Swipe of the Week' features Vincent James' '12-Month Millionaire' sales letter, noted for its impactful proof opening and huge claims followed by proof points. 'What I'm Tellin' My Team' focuses on the importance of having a clear purpose and objective for every line in marketing copy, using a sales letter for a partnership offer as an example. The 'Rockstar Resource' is a 2-minute clip of Todd Brown discussing entrepreneurial growth and overcoming fears. The 'Pic of the Week' shows his dog, Carly, after playing under a blanket.

This issue starts with a video showcasing Todd Brown's collection of physical mailings from direct response marketers over 10 years, hinting at a special project related to this collection. 'My Favs This Week' covers articles on Uber's $0 marketing strategy, belief bias in decision-making, and how Seth Godin's AltMBA achieves a 96% online course completion rate. There's another reminder about the upcoming Copy Workshop. The 'Swipe of the Week' is an 8-page sales letter by TJ Rohleder for a business opportunity, emphasizing simple language, a blind solution with benefits, and extensive proof. 'What I'm Tellin' My Team' focuses on the High-Ticket Value Equation, explaining that value for premium products is determined by the magnitude of the problem it solves, not just the quantity of features. The 'Rockstar Resource' is a podcast discussion with Joel Erway on marketing topics like unique mechanisms and offer awareness. The 'Pic of the Week' shows a rainbow in Todd Brown's backyard.

This memo discusses the importance of taking action on knowledge gained from reading non-fiction, stating that without action, reading is just entertainment. 'My Favs This Week' includes a summary of 'The Great Mental Models Volume 1' by Farnam Street, a guide to knowledge sharing for managers, and an inspiring TEDx talk by Sean Stephenson titled 'The prison of your mind.' The issue also promotes the upcoming multi-day A-Z Copywriting Workshop. The 'Swipe of the Week' is a full-page biz-op ad from about three decades ago, highlighting how the opportunity is presented as simple, easy, and fast, and the language used to build confidence in results. 'What I'm Tellin' My Team' dissects an example of 'bad copy' from a VSL, emphasizing that adjectives don't make good copy, verbs do, and that superlatives are not persuasive. 'My Advice' discusses that customer acquisition is more about economics and Average Order Value (AOV) than sales conversion rate. The 'Rockstar Resource' is Beacon, a lead magnet creator tool. The 'Pic of the Week' shows a group of online entrepreneurs at an E5 Method Workshop.

This issue focuses on Todd Brown's upcoming two-day A-Z Complete Copywriting Method Workshop, highlighting his extensive preparation and belief that it will be the only copywriting training attendees will ever need. He explains that most other trainings only cover high-level concepts, while his will delve into the 'nitty-gritty details' of sentence structure, word choice, psychological triggers, and objection-killers. 'My Favs This Week' includes articles on cutting a commonly used word to boost motivation, Ray Dalio's advice on knowing your weaknesses, and how to remember what you read. The 'Swipe of the Week' features the original International Living sales letter by Bill Bonner, noted as a great example of an Indirect Lead and how it draws readers into the setting. 'What I'm Tellin' My Team' discusses a common mistake in VSL scripts that leads to confusing copy, using an example sentence to illustrate ambiguity. 'My Advice' features a response from E5 Adviser, Paul Mascetta, on a marketing question. The 'Rockstar Resource' is Audiblogs, a tool for listening to audio versions of articles and books. The 'Pic of the Week' shows Todd Brown moving his younger daughter into her college dorm.

workshops.title: A-Z Copywriting Workshop
workshops.summary: The workshop covers strategies such as psychological triggers, framing techniques, positioning patterns, and objection-handling maneuvers.

podcast.title: The Marketer's Mind with Todd Brown
